data.description:
## Why

We want authenticated-authorship ENFORCED for this project (schema default stays OFF
for downstream clients). Flipping `identity.require_authenticated=true` ALONE is a
footgun: with it on, the write-path refuses any write it cannot sign on non-exempt
types (task/story/bug/epic), and this environment currently has NO resolvable identity
(`_seam.py:318-325`) and NO signing key — so every normal ticket write would fail
(`CommandError`). The local prerequisites are per-clone (git-ignored `.rebar/current_identity`
pointer + a private key that must not be committed), so a committed `rebar.toml` flip
cannot supply them. This story does the setup so the flip is safe.

## What

Sequence (do NOT flip the flag until steps 1-4 hold):
1. Create an `identity` ticket for each agent identity that writes here (email + SSH
   public key); `rebar identity use <id>` to write each clone's `.rebar/current_identity`.
   Note the bot email `joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` matches no identity today.
2. Configure `identity.signing_key` (or `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY`) per-clone to the
   SSH PRIVATE key — local/per-machine, never committed. Document in
   `docs/local-dev-env.md` / the identity doc.
3. Set the grandfathering boundary to the CURRENT tickets-branch HEAD so historical
   unsigned events are reported-not-enforced: set the CI `ENFORCE_SINCE` GitHub Actions
   repo variable (the workflow reads `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE`, NOT `identity.enforce_since`),
   and set `identity.enforce_since` in `rebar.toml` for local runs.
4. Confirm the `verify-identity` CI workflow actually mounts the `tickets` store (it
   currently only checks out the code repo → would exit 2 "store not found"). CI already
   hard-forces `--require-authenticated` regardless of config, so verify current gate state.
5. THEN set `identity.require_authenticated = true` in `rebar.toml`. Schema default in
   `_config_schema.py:316` stays False.

## Scope

Files: `_config_schema.py:306-329`, `_commands/_seam.py:264-325`,
`_commands/verify_authorship.py:456-544`, `.github/workflows/verify-identity.yaml`,
`docs/identity.md`, `docs/adr/0045-authenticated-identity.md`, `rebar.toml`.
This is an ACTIVATION (with prerequisites), not a prune.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each writing clone has a resolvable self-identity and a configured signing key; a signed write succeeds and an unsignable one fails as designed.
- [ ] `enforce_since` boundary set (CI repo var + local config) at the tickets HEAD current when the flag is enabled; historical unsigned events are NOT retroactively failed.
- [ ] [operator-attested] The `verify-identity` CI job mounts the tickets store and passes green with enforcement on (name the run/commit).
- [ ] `identity.require_authenticated = true` in `rebar.toml`; schema default remains False; normal agent ticket writes still succeed in this checkout.
data.title: Enable authenticated-authorship for this project (sequenced setup, then flip)
